perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information does not include information t hat (i) was in the Receiving Party’s possession prior to receiving the Confidential Information from the Disclosing Party; or (ii) is or later becomes generally available to the public through no fault of the Receiving Party; or (iii) Receiving Party devel ops internally without benefit of or reference to Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information (burden of proving independent development shall be on the Receiving Party) ; or (iv) Receiving Party receives from a third party who has the right to disclose suc h information.
perturbation.changed_text: Proprietary Data is excluded when (i) was in the Receiving Party’s possession prior to receiving the Confidential Information from the Disclosing Party; or (ii) is or later becomes generally available to the public through no fault of the Receiving Party; or (iii) Receiving Party devel ops internally without benefit of or reference to Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information (burden of proving independent development shall be on the Receiving Party) ; or (iv) Receiving Party receives from a third party who has the right to disclose suc h information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the intro word, makes this definition apply to 'Proprietary Data' rather than 'Confidential Information'. This creates a direct conflict because the document relies on the definition of 'Confidential Information' in other sections. Now we need to understand if, the exclusion apllies to 'Confidential Information' or 'Proprietary Data'. The section 7 is affected, because they make reference to what to do with the Confidential Information
perturbation.contradicted_text: The provisions of this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect beginning as of the Effective Date and shall continue with respect to a Receiving Party for as long as it retains the Confidential Information in its possession and for three (3) years thereafter. A receiving Party shall return or destroy all copies of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information to the Disclo sing Party within ten (10) days after the Disclosing Party’s request.
perturbation.justification: The change modifies the definition, now applying the exclusion to 'Proprietary Data' instead of 'Confidential Information'. As the explanation states, this creates a direct conflict with section that refer to the obligations regarding 'Confidential Information'. The Contradicted Text outlines specific obligations regarding the handling and return of 'Confidential Information'. Because the definition of the term has been modified, this affects the Contradicted Section, creating a strong in-text contradiction based on misaligned terminology.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees (i) to adopt measures to protect the confidentiality, limited use and proprietary nature of the Confidential Information at least as restrictive as those it adopts for its own confidential information of similar importance and in any event, no less than reasonable; (ii) to disclose Confidential Information to only those of its directors, officers, agents, employees, corporate affiliates and third parties retained by the Receiving Party who have a need to know such information in order for the Receiving Party to accomplish the purposes set forth in Paragraph 4 hereof and who are made aware of the confidentiality provisions of this Agreement ( and in the case of a third party, who have signed a binding undertaking of confidentiality and non -use substantially equivalent to this Agreement ), and (iii) to not use Confidential Information for any purpose except as permitted by Paragraph 4. A Receiving Party shall be responsible to the Disclosing Party for any unauthorized u se or disclosure of Confidential Information by any party to whom Receiving Party has disclosed such information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party agrees (i) to adopt measures to protect the confidentiality, limited use and proprietary nature of the Confidential Data at least as restrictive as those it adopts for its own confidential information of similar importance and in any event, no less than reasonable; (ii) to disclose Confidential Information to only those of its directors, officers, agents, employees, corporate affiliates and third parties retained by the Receiving Party who have a need to know such information in order for the Receiving Party to accomplish the purposes set forth in Paragraph 4 hereof and who are made aware of the confidentiality provisions of this Agreement ( and in the case of a third party, who have signed a binding undertaking of confidentiality and non -use substantially equivalent to this Agreement ), and (iii) to not use Confidential Information for any purpose except as permitted by Paragraph 4. A Receiving Party shall be responsible to the Disclosing Party for any unauthorized u se or disclosure of Confidential Information by any party to whom Receiving Party has disclosed such information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Data' in subpart (i) only creates ambiguity. Does the Receiving Party have different obligations for 'Confidential Information' (mentioned in (ii) and (iii)) versus 'Confidential Data'? The contradiction creates uncertainty about the scope of the Receiving Party's obligations throughout the entire section, especially considering unauthorized usage of 'Confidential Information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party agrees (i) to adopt measures to protect the confidentiality, limited use and proprietary nature of the Confidential Data at least as restrictive as those it adopts for its own confidential information of similar importance and in any event, no less than reasonable; (ii) to disclose Confidential Information to only those of its directors, officers, agents, employees, corporate affiliates and third parties retained by the Receiving Party who have a need to know such information in order for the Receiving Party to accomplish the purposes set forth in Paragraph 4 hereof and who are made aware of the confidentiality provisions of this Agreement ( and in the case of a third party, who have signed a binding undertaking of confidentiality and non -use substantially equivalent to this Agreement ), and (iii) to not use Confidential Information for any purpose except as permitted by Paragraph 4. A Receiving Party shall be responsible to the Disclosing Party for any unauthorized u se or disclosure of Confidential Information by any party to whom Receiving Party has disclosed such information.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Data' in subsection (i) of the Receiving Party's obligations, while the other subsections (ii) and (iii) and the last sentence still refer to 'Confidential Information', creates a clear in-text contradiction as defined by 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'. This discrepancy introduces ambiguity, making it unclear whether the Receiving Party has differing obligations regarding the protection and usage of 'Confidential Data' versus 'Confidential Information', and if an unauthorized disclosure occurs. The changed text directly conflicts with the continued use of 'Confidential Information' in other parts of the same section, leading to uncertainty about the scope of the Receiving Party's responsibilities.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party may use the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information for the sole purpose of evaluating, negotiating and, in its discr etion, entering into the proposed Transaction. A Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information pursuant to a request by an applicable regulatory authority, or if they are required by law or pursuant to an order of a court of competent jurisdiction to disclose Confidential Information, provided, however, in such event the Receiving Party shall (i) promptly notify the Disclosing Party; (ii) consult with the Disclosing Party as to the advisability of taking steps to resist or narrow such request; and ( iii) if so requested, cooperate with the Disclosing Party in seeking a protective or der or other appropriate remedy.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party may use the Disclosing Party’s Internal Data for the sole purpose of evaluating, negotiating and, in its discr etion, entering into the proposed Transaction. A Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information pursuant to a request by an applicable regulatory authority, or if they are required by law or pursuant to an order of a court of competent jurisdiction to disclose Confidential Information, provided, however, in such event the Receiving Party shall (i) promptly notify the Disclosing Party; (ii) consult with the Disclosing Party as to the advisability of taking steps to resist or narrow such request; and ( iii) if so requested, cooperate with the Disclosing Party in seeking a protective or der or other appropriate remedy.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Internal Data' at the beginning of the paragraph creates ambiguity. If the Receiving Party is restricted to using 'Internal Data' for evaluation and negotiation, but the disclosure exceptions later in the paragraph refer to 'Confidential Information', it is unclear whether those exceptions also apply to 'Internal Data' or only to something else. The contradiction makes it hard to understand if the Receiving Party can disclose the 'Internal Data' under the same conditions of 'Confidential Information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party may use the Disclosing Party’s Internal Data for the sole purpose of evaluating, negotiating and, in its discr etion, entering into the proposed Transaction. A Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information pursuant to a request by an applicable regulatory authority, or if they are required by law or pursuant to an order of a court of competent jurisdiction to disclose Confidential Information, provided, however, in such event the Receiving Party shall (i) promptly notify the Disclosing Party; (ii) consult with the Disclosing Party as to the advisability of taking steps to resist or narrow such request; and ( iii) if so requested, cooperate with the Disclosing Party in seeking a protective or der or other appropriate remedy.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Internal Data' in the first sentence, while the rest of the paragraph still refers to 'Confidential Information' when outlining disclosure exceptions, creates a contradiction. This is a 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' because the document uses multiple terms without clear definitions, leading to conflicting obligations. Specifically, it's unclear whether the exceptions for disclosing 'Confidential Information' also apply to 'Internal Data', creating ambiguity about what the Receiving Party is allowed to disclose under what circumstances. Because the Changed Text and Contradicted Text is the same it doesn't change that the modified version of the text now has this contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The provisions of this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect beginning as of the Effective Date and shall continue with respect to a Receiving Party for as long as it retains the Confidential Information in its possession and for three (3) years thereafter. A receiving Party shall return or destroy all copies of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information to the Disclo sing Party within ten (10) days after the Disclosing Party’s request.
perturbation.changed_text: The provisions of this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect beginning as of the Effective Date and shall continue with respect to a Receiving Party for as long as it retains the Sensitive Materials in its possession and for three (3) years thereafter. A receiving Party shall return or destroy all copies of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information to the Disclo sing Party within ten (10) days after the Disclosing Party’s request.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Sensitive Materials' in the first sentence, but retaining 'Confidential Information' in the second sentence creates a contradiction. It is unclear whether the retention period applies only to 'Sensitive Materials' while the return/destroy obligation applies only to 'Confidential Information'. This section's effectiveness becomes compromised by using 'Sensitive Materials' instead of 'Confidential Information'
perturbation.contradicted_text: The provisions of this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect beginning as of the Effective Date and shall continue with respect to a Receiving Party for as long as it retains the Sensitive Materials in its possession and for three (3) years thereafter. A receiving Party shall return or destroy all copies of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information to the Disclo sing Party within ten (10) days after the Disclosing Party’s request.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers consistently to 'Confidential Information' regarding both the retention period and the obligation to return/destroy. The changed text introduces 'Sensitive Materials' for the retention period while keeping 'Confidential Information' for the return/destroy obligation. Because the changed text creates an inconsistency in terminology that leads to uncertainty as to what the receiving party is obligated to retain versus return/destroy (are they the same, or different classes of information?), this represents a strong in-text contradiction that impairs the enforceability of the provision, and aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'. The contradicted text highlights the confusion arising from the inconsistent use of the two terms, as it is an exact copy of the changed text.
